heading: 9.7.14.5.3.Matrix Fragments formma.m8n8k16
content:
A warp executing `mma.m8n8k16` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m8n8k16`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.s8` / `.u8` | A vector expression containing a single `.b32` register, containing four `.s8` or `.u8` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, a2, a3 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 56](#mma-8816-a-i8).

  



[Figure: mma-8816-A-i8]
MMA .m8n8k16 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .u8/.s8 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: mma.m8n8k16
   Matrix: A (input matrix)
   Dimensions: M=8, N=8, K=16
   Data type: .u8 (unsigned 8-bit integer) or .s8 (signed 8-bit integer)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   Rows: 0 through 7 (representing 8 rows of the matrix, mapped to groups of 4 threads each)
   Columns: 0 through 15 (representing the K=16 dimension)
   Column groupings: cols 0-3, cols 4-7, cols 8-11, cols 12-15
   Total threads: T0 through T31 (32 threads = one warp)

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (per row)

   Row 0 (matrix row 0):
     Cols 0-3:   T0  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 4-7:   T1  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 8-11:  T2  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 12-15: T3  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}

   Row 1 (matrix row 1):
     Cols 0-3:   T4  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 4-7:   T5  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 8-11:  T6  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 12-15: T7  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}

   Row 2 (matrix row 2):
     Pattern continues (indicated by arrows showing continuation)
     Threads T8-T11 hold {a0, a1, a2, a3} across cols 0-3, 4-7, 8-11, 12-15 respectively

   Rows 3-6 (matrix rows 3-6):
     Indicated by ".." with arrows showing the pattern continues identically
     Threads T12-T27 follow the same pattern in groups of 4

   Row 7 (matrix row 7):
     Cols 0-3:   T28 holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 4-7:   T29 holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 8-11:  T30 holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 12-15: T31 holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}

4. GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY
   Each matrix row (0-7) is assigned to exactly 4 consecutive threads.
   Row 0 -> T0, T1, T2, T3
   Row 1 -> T4, T5, T6, T7
   Row 2 -> T8, T9, T10, T11
   Row 3 -> T12, T13, T14, T15
   Row 4 -> T16, T17, T18, T19
   Row 5 -> T20, T21, T22, T23
   Row 6 -> T24, T25, T26, T27
   Row 7 -> T28, T29, T30, T31
   Formula: thread = (matrix_row * 4) + (col_group), where col_group = 0,1,2,3 for cols 0-3, 4-7, 8-11, 12-15

5. REGISTER PACKING
   Each thread holds 4 elements labeled a0, a1, a2, a3.
   Since each element is 8-bit (.u8 or .s8) and PTX registers are 32-bit, four 8-bit values are packed into a single 32-bit register.
   Therefore each thread uses 1 register (32-bit) containing 4 packed 8-bit values: a0 in bits 7:0, a1 in bits 15:8, a2 in bits 23:16, a3 in bits 31:24.
   Each thread covers 4 consecutive K-dimension elements within its assigned column group.

6. LABEL AT BOTTOM
   %laneid:{fragments} — indicating the mapping is from lane ID (thread index within warp) to the fragment elements held by that lane.

MMA .m8n8k16 Fragment Layout for Matrix A with .u8/.s8 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: mma.m8n8k16
   Matrix: A (input matrix)
   Dimensions: M=8, N=8, K=16
   Data type: .u8 (unsigned 8-bit integer) or .s8 (signed 8-bit integer)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   Rows: 0 through 7 (representing 8 rows of the matrix, mapped to groups of 4 threads each)
   Columns: 0 through 15 (representing the K=16 dimension)
   Column groupings: cols 0-3, cols 4-7, cols 8-11, cols 12-15
   Total threads: T0 through T31 (32 threads = one warp)

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (per row)

   Row 0 (matrix row 0):
     Cols 0-3:   T0  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 4-7:   T1  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 8-11:  T2  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 12-15: T3  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}

   Row 1 (matrix row 1):
     Cols 0-3:   T4  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 4-7:   T5  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 8-11:  T6  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 12-15: T7  holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}

   Row 2 (matrix row 2):
     Pattern continues (indicated by arrows showing continuation)
     Threads T8-T11 hold {a0, a1, a2, a3} across cols 0-3, 4-7, 8-11, 12-15 respectively

   Rows 3-6 (matrix rows 3-6):
     Indicated by ".." with arrows showing the pattern continues identically
     Threads T12-T27 follow the same pattern in groups of 4

   Row 7 (matrix row 7):
     Cols 0-3:   T28 holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 4-7:   T29 holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 8-11:  T30 holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}
     Cols 12-15: T31 holds {a0, a1, a2, a3}

4. GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY
   Each matrix row (0-7) is assigned to exactly 4 consecutive threads.
   Row 0 -> T0, T1, T2, T3
   Row 1 -> T4, T5, T6, T7
   Row 2 -> T8, T9, T10, T11
   Row 3 -> T12, T13, T14, T15
   Row 4 -> T16, T17, T18, T19
   Row 5 -> T20, T21, T22, T23
   Row 6 -> T24, T25, T26, T27
   Row 7 -> T28, T29, T30, T31
   Formula: thread = (matrix_row * 4) + (col_group), where col_group = 0,1,2,3 for cols 0-3, 4-7, 8-11, 12-15

5. REGISTER PACKING
   Each thread holds 4 elements labeled a0, a1, a2, a3.
   Since each element is 8-bit (.u8 or .s8) and PTX registers are 32-bit, four 8-bit values are packed into a single 32-bit register.
   Therefore each thread uses 1 register (32-bit) containing 4 packed 8-bit values: a0 in bits 7:0, a1 in bits 15:8, a2 in bits 23:16, a3 in bits 31:24.
   Each thread covers 4 consecutive K-dimension elements within its assigned column group.

6. LABEL AT BOTTOM
   %laneid:{fragments} — indicating the mapping is from lane ID (thread index within warp) to the fragment elements held by that lane.


  Figure 56 MMA .m8n8k16 fragment layout for matrix A with `.u8`/`.s8` type

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row = groupID

  col =  (threadID_in_group * 4) + i       for ai    where i = {0,..,3}
  ```
* Multiplicand B:

  | .btype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.s8` / `.u8` | A vector expression containing a single `.b32` register, containing four `.s8` or `.u8` elements from the matrix B. | b0, b1, b2, b3 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 57](#mma-8816-b-i8).

  



[Figure: mma-8816-B-i8]
MMA .m8n8k16 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .u8/.s8 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Operation: MMA .m8n8k16
   Matrix: B (input matrix)
   Dimensions: M=8, N=8, K=16
   Data type: .u8 (unsigned 8-bit integer) or .s8 (signed 8-bit integer)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table has rows labeled by matrix row indices (0-15) and columns labeled 0, 1, 2, .. 7.
   Rows 0-15 represent the K=16 dimension of matrix B.
   Columns 0-7 represent the N=8 dimension of matrix B.
   Each cell contains a thread identifier and its associated register elements b0, b1, b2, b3.

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (complete layout)

   Rows 0-3, Col 0:   T0  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
   Rows 0-3, Col 1:   T4  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
   Rows 0-3, Col 2:   (arrow indicates continuation pattern)
   Rows 0-3, Col 7:   T28 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

   Rows 4-7, Col 0:   T1  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
   Rows 4-7, Col 1:   T5  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
   Rows 4-7, Col 7:   T29 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

   Rows 8-11, Col 0:  T2  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
   Rows 8-11, Col 1:  T6  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
   Rows 8-11, Col 7:  T30 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

   Rows 12-15, Col 0: T3  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
   Rows 12-15, Col 1: T7  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
   Rows 12-15, Col 7: T31 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

4. GROUPING PATTERN
   Threads are assigned along the N (column) dimension first, then the K (row) dimension:
   - Column 0 (N=0): T0 (rows 0-3), T1 (rows 4-7), T2 (rows 8-11), T3 (rows 12-15)
   - Column 1 (N=1): T4 (rows 0-3), T5 (rows 4-7), T6 (rows 8-11), T7 (rows 12-15)
   - Column 2 (N=2): T8 (rows 0-3), T9 (rows 4-7), T10 (rows 8-11), T11 (rows 12-15)  [implied by pattern]
   - ...continuing...
   - Column 7 (N=7): T28 (rows 0-3), T29 (rows 4-7), T30 (rows 8-11), T31 (rows 12-15)

   Thread numbering formula: Thread = (col * 4) + (row_group)
   where row_group = 0 for rows 0-3, 1 for rows 4-7, 2 for rows 8-11, 3 for rows 12-15

5. REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
   Each thread holds 4 register elements: b0, b1, b2, b3.
   Since the data type is .u8 or .s8 (8-bit values), four 8-bit values are packed into each 32-bit register.
   Each thread covers 4 consecutive rows (K dimension) within its assigned column (N dimension).
   b0 corresponds to the first row of the group, b1 to the second, b2 to the third, b3 to the fourth.
   All 4 elements (b0-b3) are packed into a single 32-bit register (4 x 8-bit = 32 bits).

6. ANNOTATION
   The figure label at the bottom reads: %laneid:{fragment}
   The arrow in column 2 indicates the pattern continues from col 0-1 through col 7 following the same thread assignment structure across all 32 lanes (T0-T31).

MMA .m8n8k16 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .u8/.s8 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Operation: MMA .m8n8k16
   Matrix: B (input matrix)
   Dimensions: M=8, N=8, K=16
   Data type: .u8 (unsigned 8-bit integer) or .s8 (signed 8-bit integer)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table has rows labeled by matrix row indices (0-15) and columns labeled 0, 1, 2, .. 7.
   Rows 0-15 represent the K=16 dimension of matrix B.
   Columns 0-7 represent the N=8 dimension of matrix B.
   Each cell contains a thread identifier and its associated register elements b0, b1, b2, b3.

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (complete layout)

   Rows 0-3, Col 0:   T0  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
   Rows 0-3, Col 1:   T4  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
   Rows 0-3, Col 2:   (arrow indicates continuation pattern)
   Rows 0-3, Col 7:   T28 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

   Rows 4-7, Col 0:   T1  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
   Rows 4-7, Col 1:   T5  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
   Rows 4-7, Col 7:   T29 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

   Rows 8-11, Col 0:  T2  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
   Rows 8-11, Col 1:  T6  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
   Rows 8-11, Col 7:  T30 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

   Rows 12-15, Col 0: T3  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
   Rows 12-15, Col 1: T7  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
   Rows 12-15, Col 7: T31 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

4. GROUPING PATTERN
   Threads are assigned along the N (column) dimension first, then the K (row) dimension:
   - Column 0 (N=0): T0 (rows 0-3), T1 (rows 4-7), T2 (rows 8-11), T3 (rows 12-15)
   - Column 1 (N=1): T4 (rows 0-3), T5 (rows 4-7), T6 (rows 8-11), T7 (rows 12-15)
   - Column 2 (N=2): T8 (rows 0-3), T9 (rows 4-7), T10 (rows 8-11), T11 (rows 12-15)  [implied by pattern]
   - ...continuing...
   - Column 7 (N=7): T28 (rows 0-3), T29 (rows 4-7), T30 (rows 8-11), T31 (rows 12-15)

   Thread numbering formula: Thread = (col * 4) + (row_group)
   where row_group = 0 for rows 0-3, 1 for rows 4-7, 2 for rows 8-11, 3 for rows 12-15

5. REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
   Each thread holds 4 register elements: b0, b1, b2, b3.
   Since the data type is .u8 or .s8 (8-bit values), four 8-bit values are packed into each 32-bit register.
   Each thread covers 4 consecutive rows (K dimension) within its assigned column (N dimension).
   b0 corresponds to the first row of the group, b1 to the second, b2 to the third, b3 to the fourth.
   All 4 elements (b0-b3) are packed into a single 32-bit register (4 x 8-bit = 32 bits).

6. ANNOTATION
   The figure label at the bottom reads: %laneid:{fragment}
   The arrow in column 2 indicates the pattern continues from col 0-1 through col 7 following the same thread assignment structure across all 32 lanes (T0-T31).


  Figure 57 MMA .m8n8k16 fragment layout for matrix B with `.u8`/`.s8` type

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =  (threadID_in_group * 4) + i         for bi    where i = {0,..,3}

  col =    groupID
  ```
* Accumulators (C or D):

  | .ctype / .dtype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.s32` | A vector expression containing of two `.s32` registers. | c0, c1 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 58](#mma-8816-c-i8).

  



[Figure: mma-8816-C-i8]
MMA .m8n8k16 Fragment Layout for Accumulator Matrix C/D with .s32 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND TYPE
   - Instruction: mma.m8n8k16
   - Matrix: C/D (accumulator)
   - Data type: .s32 (32-bit signed integer)
   - Matrix dimensions: M=8, N=8, K=16

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   - Rows (0 through 7) represent the 8 rows of the accumulator matrix
   - Columns (0 through 7) represent the 8 columns of the accumulator matrix
   - Column pairs are grouped: {0,1}, {2,3}, {4,5}, {6,7}
   - Threads T0 through T31 (32 threads total, one warp)
   - Label at bottom: %laneid:{fragments}

3. REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
   - Each thread holds 2 elements: {c0, c1}
   - Since the data type is .s32, each element occupies one full 32-bit register
   - Each thread therefore uses 2 registers for this fragment

4. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (per matrix row)

   Row 0:
     Columns 0-1: T0:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T1:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T2:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T3:  {c0, c1}

   Row 1:
     Columns 0-1: T4:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T5:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T6:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T7:  {c0, c1}

   Row 2:
     (Pattern continues with arrows indicating continuation from row 1 pattern downward)
     Threads T8-T11 follow the same column grouping pattern

   Rows 3-6:
     (Indicated by ".." — pattern continues consistently)

   Row 7:
     Columns 0-1: T28: {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T29: {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T30: {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T31: {c0, c1}

5. GROUPING PATTERN
   - Each row of the 8x8 accumulator matrix is covered by 4 threads
   - Each of those 4 threads covers 2 consecutive columns (a column pair)
   - Row 0 → Threads T0, T1, T2, T3
   - Row 1 → Threads T4, T5, T6, T7
   - Row 2 → Threads T8, T9, T10, T11
   - Row 3 → Threads T12, T13, T14, T15
   - Row 4 → Threads T16, T17, T18, T19
   - Row 5 → Threads T20, T21, T22, T23
   - Row 6 → Threads T24, T25, T26, T27
   - Row 7 → Threads T28, T29, T30, T31
   - General rule: For matrix row R, threads T(4R) through T(4R+3) hold that row
   - Within each row, thread T(4R+0) holds columns 0-1, T(4R+1) holds columns 2-3, T(4R+2) holds columns 4-5, T(4R+3) holds columns 6-7

6. ELEMENT ASSIGNMENT WITHIN EACH THREAD
   - c0 corresponds to the lower-indexed column of the pair (e.g., column 0 for T0, column 2 for T1)
   - c1 corresponds to the higher-indexed column of the pair (e.g., column 1 for T0, column 3 for T1)
   - Each of c0 and c1 is one 32-bit .s32 register (no packing; one element per register)
   - Total registers per thread for this fragment: 2

MMA .m8n8k16 Fragment Layout for Accumulator Matrix C/D with .s32 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND TYPE
   - Instruction: mma.m8n8k16
   - Matrix: C/D (accumulator)
   - Data type: .s32 (32-bit signed integer)
   - Matrix dimensions: M=8, N=8, K=16

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   - Rows (0 through 7) represent the 8 rows of the accumulator matrix
   - Columns (0 through 7) represent the 8 columns of the accumulator matrix
   - Column pairs are grouped: {0,1}, {2,3}, {4,5}, {6,7}
   - Threads T0 through T31 (32 threads total, one warp)
   - Label at bottom: %laneid:{fragments}

3. REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
   - Each thread holds 2 elements: {c0, c1}
   - Since the data type is .s32, each element occupies one full 32-bit register
   - Each thread therefore uses 2 registers for this fragment

4. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (per matrix row)

   Row 0:
     Columns 0-1: T0:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T1:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T2:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T3:  {c0, c1}

   Row 1:
     Columns 0-1: T4:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T5:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T6:  {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T7:  {c0, c1}

   Row 2:
     (Pattern continues with arrows indicating continuation from row 1 pattern downward)
     Threads T8-T11 follow the same column grouping pattern

   Rows 3-6:
     (Indicated by ".." — pattern continues consistently)

   Row 7:
     Columns 0-1: T28: {c0, c1}
     Columns 2-3: T29: {c0, c1}
     Columns 4-5: T30: {c0, c1}
     Columns 6-7: T31: {c0, c1}

5. GROUPING PATTERN
   - Each row of the 8x8 accumulator matrix is covered by 4 threads
   - Each of those 4 threads covers 2 consecutive columns (a column pair)
   - Row 0 → Threads T0, T1, T2, T3
   - Row 1 → Threads T4, T5, T6, T7
   - Row 2 → Threads T8, T9, T10, T11
   - Row 3 → Threads T12, T13, T14, T15
   - Row 4 → Threads T16, T17, T18, T19
   - Row 5 → Threads T20, T21, T22, T23
   - Row 6 → Threads T24, T25, T26, T27
   - Row 7 → Threads T28, T29, T30, T31
   - General rule: For matrix row R, threads T(4R) through T(4R+3) hold that row
   - Within each row, thread T(4R+0) holds columns 0-1, T(4R+1) holds columns 2-3, T(4R+2) holds columns 4-5, T(4R+3) holds columns 6-7

6. ELEMENT ASSIGNMENT WITHIN EACH THREAD
   - c0 corresponds to the lower-indexed column of the pair (e.g., column 0 for T0, column 2 for T1)
   - c1 corresponds to the higher-indexed column of the pair (e.g., column 1 for T0, column 3 for T1)
   - Each of c0 and c1 is one 32-bit .s32 register (no packing; one element per register)
   - Total registers per thread for this fragment: 2


  Figure 58 MMA .m8n8k16 fragment layout for accumulator matrix C/D with `.s32` type

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row = groupID

  col = (threadID_in_group * 2) + i         for ci    where i = {0, 1}
  ```
